channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he's considering buying used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday he is considering buying used Boeing aircraft — perhaps from an overseas seller — to use as Air Force One when he's aboard, as he fumes over the U.S. plane-maker's delays in producing two specially modified ones for presidential use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump backs idea to send some DOGE savings to American citizens
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that he likes the idea of giving some of the savings from Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency back to U.S. citizens as a kind of dividend.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order ending use of taxpayer money to 'incentivize or support' illegal immigration
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order ending the use of federal taxpayer money for resources supporting illegal immigration and illegal aliens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs late-night executive order abolishing handful of federal advisory boards
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order late Wednesday, eliminating a handful of federal advisory committees like the Presidio Trust and the Inter-American Foundation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans are pursuing separate paths to get Trump's priorities through Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Majority Leader John Thune teed up a vote this week on a budget plan that sets the stage for a massive boost in defense and border security spending, and leaves a looming tax cut fight for later this year.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate pushes toward confirmation of Kash Patel as FBI director
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate was set to vote Thursday on whether to confirm Kash Patel as FBI director, a decision that could place him atop the nation's premier federal law enforcement agency despite concerns from Democrats over his qualifications and the prospect that he would do President Donald
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order targeting any federal benefits going to those in the country illegally
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed an executive order intended to identify and eliminate any federal financial benefits going to immigrants who entered the country illegally, something already prohibited existing the law.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order aimed at ending federal benefits for some immigrants
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed an order aimed at ending federal benefits for people in the country illegally, the White House said Wednesday, his latest in a blizzard of moves to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says federal government should 'take over' DC, backing congressional GOP push
item.description: President Donald Trump is throwing his support behind congressional efforts for a federal takeover of the nation’s capital, saying he approves putting the District of Columbia back under direct federal control.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hegseth orders Pentagon to make plans for major budget cuts to align with Trump DOGE priorities
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has ordered the Pentagon to develop plans for cutting 8% from the Department of Defense budget.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department official urges quick dismissal of criminal case against New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary courtroom showdown, a federal judge pressed the Justice Department on Wednesday on its demand to throw out New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, grilling a top official and putting the Democratic mayor under oath to answer concerns that the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fetterman loses two top staffers as he makes waves by bucking Democratic Party
item.description: Renegade Pennsylvania Democrat John Fetterman is suffering more staff departures as they continue to color his time in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order instructing agencies to hunt down regulations that violate Constitution
item.description: The executive order will require federal agencies to submit a list to the Office of Management and Budget of regulations that could violate the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin lays out roles of 'probationary employees' as Dems claim critical employees are being cut
item.description: The Trump administration explained to Fox News Digital that probationary employees of the federal government, who are facing mass terminations, are not ensured permanent employment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP budget bill back on track hours after Trump threw it into turmoil
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Back on track, Senate Republicans pushed ahead Wednesday with their $340 billion budget bill focused on funding the White House's mass deportations and border security agenda after Vice President JD Vance gave a green light to proceed despite a morning dust-up caused by President D
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's plan to end taxes on Social Security benefits would be costly
item.description: Donald Trump wants to end federal income taxes on Social Security benefits but that would come at a steep cost.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Judge homes in on DOGE staffer duties in privacy lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday looked to pinpoint the exact duties of Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers during a hearing over whether to cut off their unfettered access to troves of sensitive personal data. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department makes 5 drug cartels terrorist organization
item.description: Tren de Aragua, MS-13 and the Sinaloa and Jalisco cartels are among those named terrorists by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kelly Loeffler to lead Small Business Administration
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed former Sen. Kelly Loeffler (R-Ga.) to lead the Small Business Administration (SBA), keeping up a frantic pace to cement President Trump's team in place. Senators voted 52-46 on Loeffler's nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: She worked for years to get a federal job. Within a month, she was fired
item.description: Rose is one of thousands of federal workers who have been terminated by the Trump administration. At 26, she’s left wondering what to do now.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel, MPR News

item.title: US top diplomat Rubio visits the UAE after landmark talks with Russia over Ukraine war
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates, wrapping up an overseas trip that saw the highest-level outreach between the United States and Russia since Moscow launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate confirms Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Howard Lutnick to serve as Commerce secretary, approving a key member of President Trump’s economic team amid rising trade tensions. Senators voted 51-45 in favor of Lutnick’s confirmation to lead the Commerce Department, which has broad authority over U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge declined to block Elon Musk’s DOGE from gaining access to Education Department data on student borrowers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: As sports betting has soared, more people search online for help with gambling addiction
item.description: A study shows more people are looking for help to manage gambling addiction, in the years after a Supreme Court decision allowed online sports betting in 38 states.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle, MPR News

item.title: New Florida Attorney General sworn in, first priority illegal immigration
item.description: Florida’s new Attorney General has been sworn in and he's hitting the ground, running. After taking the oath of office, James Uthmeier stood with Gov. Ron DeSantis and the cabinet holding an emergency meeting to implement immigration enforcement measures.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters to demand Gov. Hochul remove NYC Mayor Adams from office
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Protesters are expected to descend upon City Hall on Monday to demand Gov. Kathy Hochul remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump official considering running for California governor if Kamala Harris enters race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a Trump administration official, said Friday that he would consider entering the 2026 California governor race if former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris joins the growing list of candidates.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Could Kamala Harris be the next California governor? Here's how she's polling
item.description: Former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris holds a substantial lead over other potential candidates to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5
